name: InquirerPy (Interactive CLI Prompts)

performance.notes: Terminal I/O; latency is human-response time

agent_readiness.notes: Rich interactive CLI prompts inspired by Inquirer.js. from InquirerPy import inquirer. name = inquirer.text('Your name?').execute(). confirm = inquirer.confirm('Proceed?').execute(). choice = inquirer.select('Pick option:', choices=['a', 'b', 'c']).execute(). multi = inquirer.checkbox('Select multiple:', choices=['x', 'y', 'z']).execute(). path = inquirer.filepath('File path:').execute(). secret = inquirer.secret('Password:').execute(). Fuzzy search: inquirer.fuzzy('Select:', choices=items).execute(). Async: await inquirer.text('Name?').execute_async(). Validation: .validate(lambda x: x != ''). Default values: .default('my_default'). Style: style={'ansicyan': 'fg:cyan'}. prompt-toolkit backend.

gotchas: Blocks execution — must be in main thread with TTY
No support for non-interactive environments (pipes, CI) without workarounds
questionary is an alternative with simpler API for basic use cases
best_when: Interactive CLI tools requiring user input (setup wizards, configuration tools)
Multi-select checkboxes, fuzzy search selection from lists
File path completion and autocomplete prompts
When Inquirer.js-style prompts are wanted in Python CLIs
avoid_when: When questionary is sufficient for basic prompts
summary: InquirerPy provides Inquirer.js-style interactive CLI prompts. inquirer.text(), .select(), .checkbox(), .fuzzy(), .filepath(), .secret(). .execute() or .execute_async(). Validation, defaults, styling. prompt-toolkit backend. MIT. Best for CLI setup wizards and interactive selection from lists. Blocks main thread. No CI/pipe support. questionary is simpler alternative.
